news.title: ‘No apparent error’: Allahabad HC rejects GNIDA’s review on IT firm plot
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has rejected a review petition by Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority. The court previously overturned the cancellation of a 25-acre IT plot allotted to Elevator Properties. The Authority failed to follow a statutory deadline for issuing notices to the developer. This ruling upholds the original allotment, emphasizing procedural lapses by the Authority.

news.title: Secunderabad cantonment board announces OTS for property tax arrears
news.para: Secunderabad Cantonment Board (SCB) has launched a one-time settlement scheme for property tax arrears, offering a 30% rebate on accumulated interest or ₹10,000, whichever is higher. Property owners must pay their full outstanding dues by March 31 to avail this concession, aiming to encourage voluntary settlement and reduce long-standing liabilities.

news.title: Haryana plans to mandate registration of property sale agreement
news.para: Haryana plans to mandate registration of Agreements to Sell to enhance transparency and legal security in property deals. This move aims to curb fraud and litigation by ensuring all property commitments are formally documented, unlike current practices of using plain paper or notarized agreements with weaker legal standing.

news.title: OREAT dismisses promoter’s appeal against Odisha RERA conditions on Ghatikia project
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal upheld conditions set by ORERA for a housing project's third phase registration. The promoter cannot execute sale agreements without re-validated building plan approval or occupancy certificate. This follows a land record dispute and a previous NGT order halting construction. The tribunal emphasized protecting homebuyers, stating corrected land records are crucial for valid agreements and possession.

news.title: MahaRERA has over 6,300 unresolved complaints as of December 2025
news.para: Maharashtra Real Estate Regulatory Authority (MahaRERA) has 6,366 unresolved complaints as of December 2025, despite efforts to reduce the backlog. While the authority has resolved over 26,000 complaints since its inception, consumer bodies urge faster redressal mechanisms to address ongoing delays and ensure effective dispute resolution.

news.title: Pay back excess power charges to residents: Panel to RWA & Unitech Realty in Gurugram
news.para: Haryana Electricity Regulatory Commission (HERC) has ordered Uniworld Gardens II RWA and Unitech Realty to refund extra electricity charges collected from residents and imposed a Rs 50,000 penalty. The commission barred Unitech from using a Supreme Court moratorium to evade billing rules, emphasizing compliance with tariff regulations.

news.title: Commercial property turmoil in USA weighs on German banks
news.para: German property financiers Deutsche Pfandbriefbank and Aareal Bank reported disappointing financial outcomes for 2025. The duo highlighted severe challenges in the US commercial real estate industry as a major factor. Deutsche Pfandbriefbank grappled with a substantial net loss, prompting their exit from the American market.

news.title: UAE's property sector faces reckoning after Iran strikes
news.para: The Iranian missile strikes have sent shockwaves through the UAE's property sector, unveiling its vulnerability due to dependence on foreign investments. Investors are now reevaluating their commitments, resulting in a transformed market landscape for developers in Dubai and Abu Dhabi. Moving forward, the stability and appeal of the property market will rely heavily on foreign interest and regional peace.

news.title: Nexus Select Mall Management settles allegations of REITs violation with Sebi
news.para: Nexus Select Mall Management has resolved allegations of breaching REIT guidelines by settling with Sebi. The firm has agreed to pay Rs 24.37 lakh, addressing concerns over its inability to uphold essential net worth requirements and notify Sebi of pertinent alterations.

news.title: British homebuilder Taylor Wimpey warns on profit as margin pressures deepen
news.para: Taylor Wimpey is bracing for a dip in profits this year as construction costs soar and pricing becomes more competitive, squeezing their margins. However, they are optimistic for a rebound in the latter part of 2026. Despite recent government reforms aimed at boosting housing accessibility, many buyers are still grappling with affordability challenges.

news.title: MHADA signs redevelopment agreements for GTB Nagar project in Mumbai
news.para: The 11.2-acre redevelopment project at Sion Koliwada will provide new homes to around 1,200 eligible residents through MHADA’s first construction-and-development model on private land.

news.title: CCI clears Bagmane Prime Office REIT’s acquisition of stake in Bagmane Developers, other entities
news.para: The competition watchdog approved the proposed transaction involving the acquisition of Bagmane Developers and related entities by Bagmane Prime Office REIT, along with the transfer of assets and issuance of REIT units to existing shareholders.

news.title: Embassy REIT raises ₹1,400 crore through 10-year NCD issue
news.para: Embassy REIT has locked in ₹1,400 crore through a 10-year bond sale, enhancing its debt maturity timeline. The initiative saw full subscription from a leading life insurance player, making it the REIT's second successful 10-year bond issuance this fiscal year.

news.title: Barratt Redrow CEO announces retirement, Dean Banks named as successor
news.para: Barratt Redrow has made a significant announcement with the appointment of Dean Banks as the incoming chief executive, effective late 2026. Until March 2027, David Thomas will assist in the transition, ensuring stability amidst fluctuating market conditions facing UK homebuilders.

news.title: Nashik civic body brings 30,000 new properties under tax net
news.para: The Nashik Municipal Corporation has added approximately 30,000 new properties to its tax rolls, pushing the total above 6 lakh. This retrospective assessment, covering at least two previous financial years, has generated an additional Rs30 crore. The civic body anticipates earning Rs15 crore annually from these newly taxed units.

news.title: IISc study flags groundwater crisis in Bengaluru; BWSSB readies ₹10 crore summer plan
news.para: Bengaluru faces a looming groundwater crisis, with 65 wards reporting alarming depletion. A Rs 10-crore plan is underway to mitigate potential summer shortages, including intensified borewell monitoring and ensuring optimal functioning of RO plants. The city water supply agency is also prioritizing new connections and repairing damaged pipelines to address the issue.

news.title: Over 75% Mihan PAPs given developed plots: Maharashtra CM
news.para: Developed plots are being handed over to Mihan project-affected persons in Nagpur district. Over 75% have received their plots. More than Rs100 crore has been paid to 2,048 individuals as compensation for promised employment. Land acquisition for the Mihan project began in 1997. Farmers were promised compensation, plots, and jobs.

news.title: Karnataka high court rules property buyer entitled to khata transfer
news.para: A property buyer under the SARFAESI Act is entitled to a khata transfer, the high court ruled. The court noted that a sale certificate under the Act carries a presumption of regularity. It directed the restoration of power supply and consideration of the khata transfer application, stating that alleged unauthorized construction couldn't justify khata cancellation beyond statutory limitations.

news.title: Maharashtra government scraps NA tax for urban housing societies, waives all dues
news.para: Maharashtra government has abolished the annual non-agricultural tax for urban housing societies. This decision offers significant financial relief to thousands of flat owners across the state. All pending dues and arrears have been waived. The move simplifies procedures and resolves long-standing disputes. This change applies to all urban residential properties, old and new.
